message:
fix(storage): unbreak sync_fts_for_pane call site + index seq=0 on first sync (ft-7do6c)

## Defect: broken build + seq=0 off-by-one

Commit 49937d56 (ft-5n9my) bundled a half-done version of my
ft-7do6c fix: the `get_unindexed_segments_sync` function signature
gained a new `include_from_zero: bool` parameter, and the docstring
was updated to explain the ft-7do6c rationale, but the single
call-site in `sync_fts_for_pane` at storage.rs:16004 was NOT
updated — it still passes only 4 arguments to the 5-arg function.
HEAD cargo check on frankenterm-core currently fails with an E0061
argument-count mismatch at that call-site; anyone who pulls main
can't compile the crate.

That half-done state also leaves the underlying ft-7do6c defect
open: `append_segment_sync` at storage.rs:12355 assigns the first
segment of every fresh pane seq=0 via
`COALESCE(MAX(seq) + 1, 0)`, and `sync_fts_for_pane`'s
`last_indexed_seq = progress.map_or(0, …)` defaults to 0 for a
never-synced pane. The pre-existing strict-`>` filter turned that
into `WHERE seq > 0`, silently skipping the seq=0 segment forever.
Under deferred-FTS mode (ft-wk5fo), the pane's first captured
output — command prompts, shell startup, first workflow output —
was never searchable via FTS.

## Fix

Two coupled changes here, both inside sync_fts_for_pane:

1. Track `had_prior_progress = progress.is_some()` as the
   "never indexed" sentinel, since a progress row of
   `last_indexed_seq = 0` is ambiguous between "nothing indexed yet"
   and "seq=0 indexed successfully".

2. In the batch-fetch loop, compute
   `include_from_zero = !had_prior_progress && total_indexed == 0`
   and pass it to `get_unindexed_segments_sync`. Only the very
   first batch of the very first sync uses the inclusive
   `WHERE pane_id = ?1` variant (covering seq=0). As soon as
   anything is indexed in this call (`total_indexed > 0`) or a
   prior run already wrote progress (`had_prior_progress`), we
   revert to the strict `seq > max_seq` variant so we don't
   re-index rows we just handled.

The inclusive-query branch was already added by 49937d56; this
commit supplies the call-site update that makes the whole machinery
compile, plus the regression test that proves the defect is gone.

## Regression test

`ft_7do6c_catchup_indexes_seq_zero_on_first_sync` (fts_sync_tests):

  - Fresh in-memory DB + apply_defer_fts_triggers.
  - Insert 5 segments for pane 1 with explicit seq 0..=4
    (the real-world shape the ft-wk5fo family skipped).
  - Pre-catchup: fts_match_count("seqzero") == 0
    (deferred mode ⇒ FTS untouched by inserts).
  - Run sync_fts_on_startup. Assert:
      * segments_indexed == 5 (pre-fix would be 4, skipping seq=0)
      * fts_match_count("seqzero") == 5
      * fts_match_count("\"seqzero-0\"") == 1 — explicitly the
        seq=0 row is searchable, not just some permutation that
        sums to 5.
  - Resume invariant: second catchup is a no-op, FTS count
    stays at 5. Proves progress is correctly bumped to
    last_indexed_seq=4 after the first call, so subsequent
    calls hit the strict `seq > 4` branch.

The resume check is what would fail if the include_from_zero
branch wrongly ran on every batch — we'd re-index rows and
FTS would gain duplicates. Pins the "first batch of first sync
only" contract from the caller side.

## Verification

frankenterm-core --lib currently has pre-existing unrelated
blockers (ft-kqidb / ft-uigpj / ft-s2u51 / ft-zv3u9). With this
commit, the ft-5n9my-introduced compile error is gone — the
fts_sync_tests module will exercise the new regression alongside
the existing 6 ft-wk5fo tests once any of the four other blockers
lands. The edit is otherwise purely additive at the call site
(one function call gains two arguments; no new public surface,
no schema change, no new field).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
